reasoning: The long course 100m breaststroke world record of 1:04.13 by Lilly King has stood since 2017. The given time window from October 2025 to January 24, 2026 is entirely outside the typical long-course championship peak season (which occurs in spring and summer). Elite competitions during these late-fall and winter months are predominantly swum in short course (25m) format (e.g., Short Course World Championships) or yards. Because top swimmers do not taper for long course events during this part of the year, the likelihood of a major long-course world record being broken is essentially zero. Combined with the sheer difficulty of the 1:04.13 mark, King is almost guaranteed to retain her record through January 2026.
